Preliminary estimates for the cost and timeline of the rebuild were made less than two weeks after the initial incident in March 2024.

Help protect boaters, marine workers and construction personnel during the rebuilding of the Key Bridge on the Patapsco River."},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Francis_Scott_Key_Bridge_replacement","title":"Francis Scott Key Bridge replacement - Wikipedia","content":"Six weeks after the collapse, officials at the Maryland Department of Transportation announced plans to replace the bridge by October 2028 at an estimated cost of $1.7 billion to $1.9 billion.
In April 2026, the state abruptly cut ties with the design contractor Kiewit.
In the early morning of March 26, 2024, the Francis Scott Key Bridge collapsed after the container ship Dali struck one of its piers.
The report also noted that the I-35W Saint Anthony Falls Bridge that replaced the I-35W Mississippi River bridge was completed in 11 months with the help of a CE, while repairs to the Sunshine Skyway Bridge took five years to complete.
Petersburg, Florida; it would have a central span of 2,300 feet (700 m), twice that of the old bridge, and rise 213 feet (65 m), compared to the previous 185 feet (56 m).
On August 1, 2024, the state rejected Archer Western's proposal as uncompliant with the contract's requirements.
On August 29, 2024, the Maryland Transportation Authority awarded a $73 million contract to Kiewit for pre-construction and design work.
On November 17, 2025, Maryland officials announced that the anticipated opening date had been delayed two years to late 2030, and the projected cost had more than doubled, to an estimated $4.3 billion to $5.2 billion.
But in November, MTA officials pushed it to 2025 and said it was expected to take about 10 months.
